verses.text: The LORD to Moses: this is the land of Canaan with its borders. South border: from the wilderness of Zin along by Edom, from the Salt Sea eastward; turning at the ascent of Akrabbim, through Zin, Kadesh-barnea, Hazar-addar, Azmon, the river of Egypt; outgoings at the sea. Guidance: God draws the property line before they cross. Specific landmarks — a sea, a mountain pass, a wadi. The promise is not vague; it is mapped. Faith inherits real ground, not just a feeling.

The LORD to Moses: this is the land of Canaan with its borders. South border: from the wilderness of Zin along by Edom, from the Salt Sea eastward; turning at the ascent of Akrabbim, through Zin, Kadesh-barnea, Hazar-addar, Azmon, the river of Egypt; outgoings at the sea. Guidance: God draws the property line before they cross. Specific landmarks — a sea, a mountain pass, a wadi. The promise is not vague; it is mapped. Faith inherits real ground, not just a feeling.

West border: the great sea — the Mediterranean. North border: from the great sea point out mount Hor; from mount Hor to the entrance of Hamath; on to Zedad, Ziphron; goings out at Hazar-enan. Guidance: The Mediterranean to the west, a mountain range to the north. Israel's house has walls — a sea on one side, mountains on the other. A people called out of slavery gets a country with edges they can stand on.

West border: the great sea — the Mediterranean. North border: from the great sea point out mount Hor; from mount Hor to the entrance of Hamath; on to Zedad, Ziphron; goings out at Hazar-enan. Guidance: The Mediterranean to the west, a mountain range to the north. Israel's house has walls — a sea on one side, mountains on the other. A people called out of slavery gets a country with edges they can stand on.

East border: from Hazar-enan to Shepham; down to Riblah on the east side of Ain; reaching the side of the sea of Chinnereth eastward; down to Jordan, goings out at the Salt Sea. This is your land with its borders round about. Guidance: The Sea of Galilee, the Jordan flowing down, the Salt Sea at the south — the eastern fence anchored by water. The map closes at the same Salt Sea where it opened. Israel's land is a circle, not an arrow; it has a home, not just a heading.

Moses to Israel: this is the land ye shall inherit by lot, which the LORD commanded for the nine tribes and the half tribe. For Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh have received their inheritance on this side Jordan eastward. Guidance: Two-and-a-half east, nine-and-a-half west. The covenant accounts for both. No tribe is lost in the math; everyone has a place. God's family table makes room for the early-claimers without forgetting the latecomers.

The LORD: these are the men who shall divide the land — Eleazar the priest, Joshua son of Nun, and one prince of every tribe. Judah: Caleb. Simeon: Shemuel. Benjamin: Elidad. Dan: Bukki. Manasseh: Hanniel. Ephraim: Kemuel. Zebulun: Elizaphan. Issachar: Paltiel. Asher: Ahihud. Naphtali: Pedahel. These the LORD commanded. Guidance: Ten new names — and one familiar one. Caleb, the spy who saw giants and called them bread, is still on God's payroll forty years later. Faithfulness does not retire. The men who divide the land are not appointed by lottery; they are appointed by Heaven.
